channel.title: Scott A. Nichols | Policy Analyst (sociology, literacy, and psychology
channel.description: Recent content on Scott A. Nichols | Policy Analyst (sociology, literacy, and psychology

item.title: About
item.description: I’m passionate about helping institutions and governments improve policy and evaluate program effectiveness through research. My current areas of interest are: Educational sociology and policy Equality in education with and for all populations Quantitative and qualitative methodologies Critical literacy Ayda Nichols I lost my wife, Ayda Nichols, to metastatic non-small cell lung cancer in 2019. The cancer was aggressive at stage four from day one and she fought with all her strength for seven years.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Some of the projects I was proud to be a part of. 2018 My colleagues and I presented a working paper on advocacy, care, and transformation at the Literacy Research Association convention in Indian Wells, California. ASPiRE Research Fellowship 2016 - 2019 Graduate Research Fellowship, National Science Foundation 2010 - 2012 Worked as a second grade teacher in an international school in Colombia for two years. I taught Science, Math, Social Studies, and English.

item.title: Publications
item.description: (2019). Diverse Learners: Challenges in the Field. (2018). Development of reading motivation: Towards an understanding through an examination of self-regulation and affective theory. (2018). Perspectives and policies effecting diverse learners. (2018). Achievement testing and learning strategies. A look at methodology. Stefanski, A., Kwangok, S., Fife-Demski, V., & Nichols, S. A. (2018). Advocacy, care, and transformation: A review of community-engaged scholarship in literacy research. Paper presented at the Literacy Research Association, Indian Wells, CA.
